body.title: Granny: stale-dispatched query fails, text vs timestamptz cast
body.result: ::timestamptz cast on updated_at in both stale queries; stale cleanup now runs without error
body.intention: I intend that Granny's stale-dispatched and stale-inprogress cleanup queries run without error by correctly casting the updated_at text field to timestamptz.
body.description:
Granny's stale-dispatched and stale-inprogress SQL queries fail with: `operator does not exist: text < timestamp with time zone` on `updated_at`. The updated_at column in clan.memories is stored as text; the queries compare it directly with `now() - interval '...'`. Fix: add explicit cast `updated_at::timestamptz` in the WHERE clause.

**Affected files:** devices/granny/daemon.py (stale-dispatched and stale-inprogress queries)

**Design rules:** none apply — local query fix

**Scope boundary:** Add ::timestamptz cast to both queries. Out of scope: schema migration, changing retention logic.

**Completion criteria:** Granny log shows no 'operator does not exist: text < timestamp' warnings; stale cleanup runs without error.
